case_title: JEFFREY JAMES SIRIVATH VS. MA DIGNA BAUTISTA JESUS ET AL

docket_entries.description: ACKNOWLEDGMENT OF SATISFACTION OF JUDGMENT, FULLY SATISFIED, AS TO DEFENDANT JESUS, MA DIGNA BAUTISTA
SERVICE OF COMPLAINT BY CERTIFIED MAIL FILED BY PLAINTIFF SIRIVATH, JEFFREY JAMES AS TO DEFENDANT JESUS, MA DIGNA BAUTISTA
STIPULATION FOR HEARING BEFORE PRO TEM JUDGE DAVID LAVINE. FILED BY SIRIVATH, JEFFREY JAMES JESUS, MA DIGNA BAUTISTA

THE COURT HAS ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF SIRIVATH, JEFFREY JAMES RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT JESUS, MA DIGNA BAUTISTA $750.00 PRINCIPAL, $65.00 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $815.00, PROPER VENUE WAIVED BY DEFENDANT. BAUTISTA, TERESITA JESUS, SECOND DEFENDANT DISMISSED AT REQUEST OF PLAINTIFF. THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE WAS FAULT ON BOTH SIDES. DEFENDANT'S CAR SHOULD NOT HAVE BEEN WAITING IN A LANE OF TRAFFIC, AND PLAINTIFF SHOULD HAVE SLOWED UPON APPROACH. PARTIAL DAMAGES ARE AWARDED TO PLAINTIFF. STAY OF EXECUTION 30 DAYS PLAINTIFF'S AND DEFENDANT'S EXHIBITS RECEIVED
(SC-108) ANSWER TO REQUEST TO CORRECT OR CANCEL JUDGMENT AND ANSWER (SMALL CLAIMS) FILED BY DEFENDANT BAUTISTA, TERESITA
(SC-108A) ORDER ON REQUEST TO CORRECT OR CANCEL JUDGMENT (SMALL CLAIMS) THE COURT MAKES THE FOLLOWING ORDERS; [X] THE REQUEST IS DENIED.
PROOF OF SERVICE FILED ON CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF - SIGNED SERVED APR-21-2016, COURT CERTIFIED MAIL RETURNED SERVED AS TO DEFENDANT JESUS, MA DIGNA BAUTISTA

SMALL CLAIMS CALENDAR OF JUN-10-2016 - TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS OF JUN-10-2016 BY JUDGE PRO TEM DAVID LAVINE. PLAINTIFF WAS PRESENT. DEFENDANT WAS PRESENT. PLAINTIFFS EXHIBITS RETAINED. DEFENDANTS EXHIBITS RETAINED. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID LAVINE. CLERK: P. NATT. NOT REPORTED. (506)
SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF JUN-10-2016 - JUDGMENT.

AUTO ACCIDENT CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF FOR $3,174.84 FILED BY SIRIVATH, JEFFREY JAMES AS TO DEFENDANT JESUS, MA DIGNA BAUTISTA BAUTISTA, TERESITA ORDER SETTING TRIAL ISSUED FOR JUN-10-2016 AT 03:30 PM IN DEPT 506
DISMISSAL WITHOUT PREJUDICE OF CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF FILED BY PLAINTIFF SIRIVATH, JEFFREY JAMES AS TO DEFENDANT BAUTISTA, TERESITA
